notes: v0 supported scope: ONE species (Escherichia coli), 5 well-documented antibiotics spanning distinct mechanisms. Coverage is deliberately narrow and honest — see README.

panel.name: Ampicillin
panel.notes: Aminopenicillin. Inactivated by most acquired beta-lactamases (e.g. blaTEM-1, blaSHV-1) as well as ESBLs/AmpC/carbapenemases.

panel.name: Ceftriaxone
panel.notes: Third-generation cephalosporin. Resistance driven by ESBLs (blaCTX-M, ESBL blaSHV/blaTEM), plasmid AmpC (blaCMY/blaDHA), and carbapenemases. A plain penicillinase (subclass BETA-LACTAM) does NOT confer resistance here.

panel.name: Ciprofloxacin
panel.notes: Fluoroquinolone. High-level resistance from QRDR point mutations in gyrA/parC; plasmid-mediated qnr / aac(6')-Ib-cr / oqxAB / qepA give lower-level resistance.

panel.name: Gentamicin
panel.notes: Aminoglycoside. Resistance from modifying enzymes assigned subclass GENTAMICIN by AMRFinderPlus, and from 16S rRNA methyltransferases (armA/rmt) that confer high-level pan-aminoglycoside resistance.

panel.name: Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole
panel.notes: Combination drug. v0 rule: acquired trimethoprim resistance (dfr) is treated as the primary driver of clinical co-trimoxazole failure; a sul gene is recorded as supporting evidence. Documented simplification — confirm with lab MIC.
